prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The sampled frames show a low-resolution, animated commercial with black side borders and a “Nostalgic X’er” watermark at the lower right.

At the start, the image shows an old-style television set. On the TV screen is a title card reading “ALICE IN REXALLLAND” in decorative lettering, suggesting the commercial is framed as a story or cartoon segment.

The next sampled scene shifts to a dark blue animated background. A small cartoon figure, apparently a girl-like character with light-colored hair or a cap and a red lower garment, is shown suspended or moving across the frame. The exact action is unclear from the still frame, but the figure appears to be in mid-motion against a plain blue setting.

Later, the commercial cuts to a close-up of a promotional sign or poster. Large text reads “1¢ SALE,” with “REXALL” visible near the top. The layout suggests an advertisement for a Rexall one-cent sale. Part of a cartoon character’s leg or foot is visible at the left edge, but the full character is not shown in this frame.

By the final frame, the commercial presents a large red Rexall logo on a pale yellow background. A small portion of a cartoon character’s head or hair peeks from behind the top of the logo. The frame functions visually like an end card or brand display, emphasizing the Rexall name.
